      "source_statement_or_chunk": [
        {

          "line_range": [
            663,

          "line_range_adjustment_reasons": [
            "expanded_backward_to_include_environment_begin",
            "expanded_forward_to_close_environment"
          ],
          "labels": [
            "lem.det.minors-diag"
          ],
          "snippet": "\\begin{lemma}\n\\label{lem.det.minors-diag}Let $n\\in\\mathbb{N}$. Let $d_{1},d_{2},\\ldots\n,d_{n}\\in K$. Let%\n\\[\nD:=\\left(  d_{i}\\left[  i=j\\right]  \\right)  _{1\\leq i\\leq n,\\ 1\\leq j\\leq\nn}=\\left(\n\\begin{array}\n[c]{cccc}%\nd_{1} & 0 & \\cdots & 0\\\\\n0 & d_{2} & \\cdots & 0\\\\\n\\vdots & \\vdots & \\ddots & \\vdots\\\\\n0 & 0 & \\cdots & d_{n}%\n\\end{array}\n\\right)  \\in K^{n\\times n}%\n\\]\nbe the diagonal $n\\times n$-matrix with diagonal entries $d_{1},d_{2}%\n,\\ldots,d_{n}$. Then: \\medskip\n\n\\textbf{(a)} We have $\\det\\left(  \\operatorname*{sub}\\nolimits_{P}%\n^{P}D\\right)  =\\prod_{i\\in P}d_{i}$ for any subset $P$ of $\\left[  n\\right]\n$. \\medskip\n\n\\textbf{(b)} Let $P$ and $Q$ be two distinct subsets of $\\left[  n\\right]  $\nsatisfying $\\left\\vert P\\right\\vert =\\left\\vert Q\\right\\vert $. Then,\n$\\det\\left(  \\operatorname*{sub}\\nolimits_{P}^{Q}D\\right)  =0$.\n\\end{lemma}\n\n\\begin{proof}\n[Proof of Lemma \\ref{lem.det.minors-diag}.]This is \\cite[Lemma 6.163]%\n{detnotes} (slightly rewritten); see \\cite[Exercise 6.49]{detnotes} for a\ndetailed proof. (That said, it is almost obvious: In part \\textbf{(a)}, the\nsubmatrix $\\operatorname*{sub}\\nolimits_{P}^{P}D$ is itself diagonal, and its\ndiagonal entries are precisely the $d_{i}$ for $i\\in P$. In part \\textbf{(b)},\nthe submatrix $\\operatorname*{sub}\\nolimits_{P}^{Q}D$ has a zero row (indeed,\nfrom $\\left\\vert P\\right\\vert =\\left\\vert Q\\right\\vert $ and $P\\neq Q$, we see\nthat there exists some $i\\in P\\setminus Q$, and then the corresponding row of\n$\\operatorname*{sub}\\nolimits_{P}^{Q}D$ is zero) and thus has determinant $0$.)\n\\end{proof}\n\nLemma \\ref{lem.det.minors-diag} gives very simple formulas for minors of\ndiagonal matrices. Thus, the formula of Theorem \\ref{thm.det.det(A+B)} becomes\nsimpler when the matrix $B$ is diagonal:\n\n\\begin{theorem}\n\\label{thm.det.det(A+D)}Let $n\\in\\mathbb{N}$. Let $A$ and $D$ be two $n\\times\nn$-matrices in $K^{n\\times n}$ such that the matrix $D$ is diagonal. Let\n$d_{1},d_{2},\\ldots,d_{n}$ be the diagonal entries of the diagonal matrix $D$,\nso that%\n\\[\nD=\\left(  d_{i}\\left[  i=j\\right]  \\right)  _{1\\leq i\\leq n,\\ 1\\leq j\\leq\nn}=\\left(\n\\begin{array}\n[c]{cccc}%\nd_{1} & 0 & \\cdots & 0\\\\\n0 & d_{2} & \\cdots & 0\\\\\n\\vdots & \\vdots & \\ddots & \\vdots\\\\\n0 & 0 & \\cdots & d_{n}%\n\\end{array}\n\\right)  \\in K^{n\\times n}.\n\\]\nThen,%\n\\[\n\\det\\left(  A+D\\right)  =\\sum_{P\\subseteq\\left[  n\\right]  }\\det\\left(\n\\operatorname*{sub}\\nolimits_{P}^{P}A\\right)  \\cdot\\prod_{i\\in\\left[\nn\\right]  \\setminus P}d_{i}.\n\\]\n\n\\end{theorem}\n"
        }
      ],

AlgebraicCombinatorics/CauchyBinet.lean:54-54 (namespace)\nnamespace CauchyBinet\n\n-- File context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/CauchyBinet.lean:56-56 (variable)\nvariable {R : Type*} [CommRing R]\n\n-- Finset (Fin m)) :\n    Matrix (Fin U.card) (Fin V.card) R :=\n  A.submatrix (U.orderEmbOfFin rfl) (V.orderEmbOfFin rfl)\n-- Local notation support: AlgebraicCombinatorics/CauchyBinet.lean:103-106 (submatrixOfFinset_apply)\n@[simp]\ntheorem submatrixOfFinset_apply {n m : ℕ} {R : Type*} (A : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin m) R)\n    (U : Finset (Fin n)) (V : Finset (Fin m)) (i : Fin U.card) (j : Fin V.card) :\n    submatrixOfFinset A U V i j = A (U.orderEmbOfFin rfl i) (V.orderEmbOfFin rfl j) := rfl\n-- File context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/CauchyBinet.lean:99-99 (notation)\nscoped notation \"sub[\" U \",\" V \"] \" A => submatrixOfFinset A U V\n-- Local API retrieval: AlgebraicCombinatorics/CauchyBinet.lean:230-233 (cauchyBinet_square)\ntheorem cauchyBinet_square {n : Type*} [DecidableEq n] [Fintype n]\n    (A B : Matrix n n R) :\n    (A * B).det = A.det * B.det :=\n  Matrix.det_mul A B\n-- Local API retrieval: AlgebraicCombinatorics/CauchyBinet.lean:237-239 (colsSubmatrix)\nnoncomputable def colsSubmatrix {n m : ℕ} (A : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin m) R)\n    (S : Finset (Fin m)) (hcard : S.card = n) : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) R :=\n  A.submatrix id (S.orderEmbOfFin hcard)\n-- Local API retrieval: AlgebraicCombinatorics/CauchyBinet.lean:243-245 (rowsSubmatrix)\nnoncomputable def rowsSubmatrix {n m : ℕ} (B : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin n) R)\n    (S : Finset (Fin m)) (hcard : S.card = n) : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) R :=\n  B.submatrix (S.orderEmbOfFin hcard) id\n-- Local API retrieval: AlgebraicCombinatorics/CauchyBinet.lean:247-263 (det_mul_aux_nonsquare)\n/-- Helper lemma: when f is not injective, the alternating sum over permutations is 0. -/\nlemma det_mul_aux_nonsquare {n m : ℕ} {A : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin m) R} {B : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin n) R}\n    {f : Fin n → Fin m} (hf : ¬Function.Injective f) :\n    (∑ σ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n), (Equiv.Perm.sign σ : R) * ∏ i, A (σ i) (f i) * B (f i) i) = 0 := by\n  obtain ⟨i, j, hfij, hij⟩ : ∃ i j, f i = f j ∧ i ≠ j := by\n    rw [Function.Injective] at hf; push_neg at hf; exact hf\n  exact Finset.sum_involution (fun σ _ => σ * Equiv.swap i j)\n    (fun σ _ => by\n      have h1 : (∏ k, A (σ k) (f k)) = ∏ k, A ((σ * Equiv.swap i j) k) (f k) := by\n        refine Fintype.prod_equiv (Equiv.swap i j) _ _ (fun k => ?_)\n        simp only [Equiv.Perm.coe_mul, Function.comp_apply, Equiv.swap_apply_self, \n          Equiv.apply_swap_eq_self hfij]\n      have h2 : (Equiv.Perm.sign (σ * Equiv.swap i j) : R) = -(Equiv.Perm.sign σ : R) := by \n        simp [Equiv.Perm.sign_mul, Equiv.Perm.sign_swap hij]\n      simp only [h2, neg_mul, h1, Finset.prod_mul_distrib]; ring)\n    (fun σ _ _ => (not_congr Equiv.mul_swap_eq_iff).mpr hij)\n    (fun _ _ => Finset.mem_univ _) (fun σ _ => Equiv.mul_swap_involutive i j σ)\n-- Local API retrieval: AlgebraicCombinatorics/CauchyBinet.lean:376-391 (sum_injective_eq_sum_over_subsets)\nprivate lemma sum_injective_eq_sum_over_subsets {n m : ℕ} (F : (Fin n → Fin m) → R) :\n    ∑ f ∈ (Finset.univ : Finset (Fin n → Fin m)).filter Function.Injective, F f =\n    ∑ S ∈ (Finset.univ : Finset (Fin m)).powersetCard n,\n      if h : S.card = n then ∑ τ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n), F (fun i => S.orderEmbOfFin h (τ i)) else 0 := by\n  let g : (Fin n → Fin m) → Finset (Fin m) := fun f => Finset.univ.image f\n  have hg : ∀ f ∈ (Finset.univ : Finset (Fin n → Fin m)).filter Function.Injective,\n      g f ∈ (Finset.univ : Finset (Fin m)).powersetCard n := by\n    intro f hf; simp only [Finset.mem_filter, Finset.mem_univ, true_and] at hf\n    simp only [g, Finset.mem_powersetCard, Finset.subset_univ, true_and]\n    rw [Finset.card_image_of_injective _ hf]; simp\n  rw [← Finset.sum_fiberwise_of_maps_to hg F]; apply Finset.sum_congr rfl; intro S hS\n  simp only [Finset.mem_powersetCard, Finset.subset_univ, true_and] at hS; rw [dif_pos hS]\n  have h_fiber : (Finset.filter Function.Injective Finset.univ).filter (fun f => g f = S) =\n      ((Finset.univ : Finset (Fin n → Fin m)).filter Function.Injective).filter \n        (fun f => Finset.univ.image f = S) := by ext f; simp [g]\n  rw [h_fiber]; exact sum_over_image_eq_sum_perm S hS F\n-- Local API retrieval: AlgebraicCombinatorics/CauchyBinet.lean:404-437 (cauchyBinet)\ntheorem cauchyBinet {n m : ℕ} (A : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin m) R) (B : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin n) R) :\n    (A * B).det = ∑ S ∈ (Finset.univ : Finset (Fin m)).powersetCard n,\n      if h : S.card = n then\n        (colsSubmatrix A S h).det * (rowsSubmatrix B S h).det\n      else 0 := by\n  -- Expand det(AB) using Leibniz formula\n  calc (A * B).det \n      = ∑ σ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n), (Equiv.Perm.sign σ : R) * ∏ i, (A * B) (σ i) i := by \n          rw [Matrix.det_apply']\n    _ = ∑ σ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n), (Equiv.Perm.sign σ : R) * \n          ∏ i, ∑ k : Fin m, A (σ i) k * B k i := by rfl\n    _ = ∑ σ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n), (Equiv.Perm.sign σ : R) * \n          ∑ f : Fin n → Fin m, ∏ i, A (σ i) (f i) * B (f i) i := by\n        congr 1; ext σ; congr 1\n        rw [Finset.prod_univ_sum]; simp only [Fintype.piFinset_univ]\n    _ = ∑ σ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n), ∑ f : Fin n → Fin m, \n          (Equiv.Perm.sign σ : R) * ∏ i, A (σ i) (f i) * B (f i) i := by\n        congr 1; ext σ; rw [mul_sum]\n    _ = ∑ f : Fin n → Fin m, ∑ σ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n), \n          (Equiv.Perm.sign σ : R) * ∏ i, A (σ i) (f i) * B (f i) i := by\n        rw [Finset.sum_comm]\n    _ = ∑ f ∈ (Finset.univ : Finset (Fin n → Fin m)).filter Function.Injective, \n          ∑ σ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n), (Equiv.Perm.sign σ : R) * ∏ i, A (σ i) (f i) * B (f i) i := by\n        refine (Finset.sum_subset (Finset.filter_subset _ _) ?_).symm\n        intro f _ hf\n        simp only [Finset.mem_filter, Finset.mem_univ, true_and] at hf\n        exact det_mul_aux_nonsquare hf\n    _ = _ := by\n        -- Use the bijection between injective functions and (subset, permutation) pairs\n        rw [sum_injective_eq_sum_over_subsets]\n        apply Finset.sum_congr rfl; intro S hS\n        split_ifs with h\n        · exact sum_over_subset_eq_det_mul A B S h\n        · rfl\n-- Local API finsetSumFin_empty {n : ℕ} : finsetSumFin (∅ : Finset (Fin n)) = 0 := by\n  simp [finsetSumFin]\n-- Local API retrieval: AlgebraicCombinatorics/CauchyBinet.lean:484-486 (submatrixDet)\nnoncomputable def submatrixDet {n : ℕ} (A : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) R)\n    (P Q : Finset (Fin n)) (h : P.card = Q.card) : R :=\n  (A.submatrix (P.orderEmbOfFin rfl) (Q.orderEmbOfFin (h ▸ rfl))).det\n-- Local API retrieval: AlgebraicCombinatorics/CauchyBinet.lean:562-567 (permsMapping_empty_of_card_ne)\ntheorem permsMapping_empty_of_card_ne {n : ℕ} (P Q : Finset (Fin n))\n    (h : P.card ≠ Q.card) : permsMapping P Q = ∅ := by\n  simp only [permsMapping, Finset.filter_eq_empty_iff, Finset.mem_univ, true_implies]\n  intro σ heq\n  rw [← imageFinset_card σ P, heq] at h\n  exact h rfl\n-- Local API retrieval: AlgebraicCombinatorics/CauchyBinet.lean:602-627 (det_add_expand_step3)\n/-- Third step: partition the sum over σ based on σ(P) = Q. -/\ntheorem det_add_expand_step3 {n : ℕ} (A B : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) R) :\n    (A + B).det = ∑ P : Finset (Fin n), ∑ Q : Finset (Fin n),\n      ∑ σ ∈ permsMapping P Q,\n        Equiv.Perm.sign σ • ((∏ i ∈ P, A (σ i) i) * (∏ i ∈ Pᶜ, B (σ i) i)) := by\n  rw [det_add_expand_step2]\n  congr 1\n  ext P\n  -- Partition univ by Q = σ(P)\n  have huniv : (Finset.univ : Finset (Equiv.Perm (Fin n))) =\n      Finset.univ.biUnion (fun Q => permsMapping P Q) := by\n    ext σ\n    constructor\n    · intro _\n      simp only [Finset.mem_biUnion, Finset.mem_univ, true_and]\n      exact ⟨imageFinset σ P, by simp [permsMapping]⟩\n    · intro _\n      exact Finset.mem_univ σ\n  have hdisj : Set.PairwiseDisjoint ((Finset.univ : Finset (Finset (Fin n))) : Set _)\n      (fun Q => permsMapping P Q) := by\n    intro Q₁ _ Q₂ _ hne\n    simp only hdisj]\n-- Local API retrieval: AlgebraicCombinatorics/CauchyBinet.lean:631-635 (det_add_fin_two)\ntheorem det_add_fin_two (A B : Matrix (Fin 2) (Fin 2) R) :\n    (A + B).det = A.det + B.det +\n         "guidance": [
          "Match the local Lean style shown in local_lean_style.examples when it applies; prefer exact APIs and argument order already used in this file.",

        "examples": [
          "    ## Implementation\n    \n    In Mathlib, `Matrix.submatrix` takes functions for row and column selection.\n    For finite sets, we use the canonical order-preserving embedding `orderEmbOfFin`. -/\nnoncomputable def submatrixOfFinset {n m : ℕ} (A : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin m) R)\n    (U : Finset (Fin n)) (V : Finset (Fin m)) :\n    Matrix (Fin U.card) (Fin V.card) R :=\n  A.submatrix (U.orderEmbOfFin rfl) (V.orderEmbOfFin rfl)\n\n/-- Notation for submatrices: `sub[U,V] A` denotes sub_U^V A. -/\nscoped notation \"sub[\" U \",\" V \"] \" A => submatrixOfFinset A U V\n\n/-- The (i, j) entry of sub_U^V A is A_{uᵢ, vⱼ} where uᵢ is the i-th smallest element of U\n    and vⱼ is the j-th smallest element of V. -/\n@[simp]\ntheorem submatrixOfFinset_apply {n m : ℕ} {R : Type*} (A : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin m) R)\n    (U : Finset (Fin n)) (V : Finset (Fin m)) (i : Fin U.card) (j : Fin V.card) :\n    submatrixOfFinset A U V i j = A (U.orderEmbOfFin rfl i) (V.orderEmbOfFin rfl j) := rfl\n\n/-- The submatrix of the empty row set is a 0×|V| matrix. -/\ntheorem submatrixOfFinset_empty_rows {n m : ℕ} {R : Type*} (A : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin m) R)\n    (V : Finset (Fin m)) :\n    submatrixOfFinset A ∅ V = ![] := by\n  ext i _\n  exact i.elim0\n\n/-- The submatrix of the empty column set is a |U|×0 matrix. -/\ntheorem submatrixOfFinset_empty_cols {n m : ℕ} {R : Type*} (A : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin m) R)\n    (U : Finset (Fin n)) :\n    submatrixOfFinset A U ∅ = fun _ j => j.elim0 := by\n  ext _ j\n  exact j.elim0\n\n/-- The submatrix respects matrix addition. -/\ntheorem submatrixOfFinset_add {n m : ℕ} (A B : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin m) R)",
          "theorem cauchyBinet_square {n : Type*} [DecidableEq n] [Fintype n]\n    (A B : Matrix n n R) :\n    (A * B).det = A.det * B.det :=\n  Matrix.det_mul A B\n\n/-- Submatrix obtained by selecting specific columns of A.\n    `cols_g A` in the source notation selects columns indexed by g. -/\nnoncomputable def colsSubmatrix {n m : ℕ} (A : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin m) R)\n    (S : Finset (Fin m)) (hcard : S.card = n) : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) R :=\n  A.submatrix id (S.orderEmbOfFin hcard)\n\n/-- Submatrix obtained by selecting specific rows of B.\n    `rows_g B` in the source notation selects rows indexed by g. -/\nnoncomputable def rowsSubmatrix {n m : ℕ} (B : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin n) R)\n    (S : Finset (Fin m)) (hcard : S.card = n) : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) R :=\n  B.submatrix (S.orderEmbOfFin hcard) id",
          "theorem det_add_sum {n : ℕ} (A B : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) R) :\n    (A + B).det = ∑ P : Finset (Fin n), ∑ Q : Finset (Fin n),\n      if h : Q.card = P.card then\n        (-1 : R) ^ (finsetSumFin P + finsetSumFin Q) *\n        submatrixDet A Q P h *\n        submatrixDet B Qᶜ Pᶜ (by simp [Finset.card_compl, h])\n      else 0 := by\n  rw [det_add_expand_step3]\n  congr 1\n  ext P\n  congr 1\n  ext Q\n  by_cases hcard : Q.card = P.card\n  · rw [dif_pos hcard]\n    -- Use sum_perms_mapping_eq_det_product\n    have h := sum_perms_mapping_eq_det_product A B P Q hcard.symm\n    convert h using 2\n  · rw [dif_neg hcard]\n    rw [permsMapping_empty_of_card_ne P Q (fun h => hcard h.symm)]\n    simp",
          "theorem det_mul_eq_zero_of_rank_deficient {n m : ℕ} [NeZero n]\n    (A : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin m) R) (B : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin n) R) (h : m < n) :\n    (A * B).det = 0 := by\n  rw [cauchyBinet]\n  have hempty : (Finset.univ : Finset (Fin m)).powersetCard n = ∅ := by\n    rw [powersetCard_eq_empty]\n    simp only [card_univ, Fintype.card_fin]\n    exact h\n  rw [hempty]\n  simp\n\n/-!\n## Determinant of A + B (Theorem thm.det.det(A+B))\n\nFor n×n matrices A and B:\n  det(A+B) = ∑_{P ⊆ [n]} ∑_{Q ⊆ [n], |P|=|Q|} (-1)^(sum P + sum Q) · det(sub_P^Q A) · det(sub_P̃^Q̃ B)\n\nwhere P̃ denotes the complement [n] \\ P.\n\nThis is a \"binomial theorem\" for determinants, containing det(A) (P=Q=[n])\nand det(B) (P=Q=∅) as special terms.\n-/\n\n/-- Sum of elements in a finset of Fin n, viewed as natural numbers.\n    Used in the sign factor of the det(A+B) formula.\n\n    Note: This is named `finsetSumFin` to distinguish from `AlgebraicCombinatorics.QBinomialRec.finsetSumNat`,\n    which computes the sum of elements in a `Finset ℕ` directly.\n    Both compute \"the sum of elements\" but for different element types. -/\ndef finsetSumFin {n : ℕ} (P : Finset (Fin n)) : ℕ := ∑ i ∈ P, i.val\n\n/-- The sum over the empty set is 0. -/\n@[simp] lemma finsetSumFin_empty {n : ℕ} : finsetSumFin (∅ : Finset (Fin n)) = 0 := by\n  simp [finsetSumFin]\n\n/-- Helper: Given P, Q with same cardinality, compute the determinant of the\n    submatrix of A restricted to rows P and columns Q. -/\nnoncomputable def submatrixDet {n : ℕ} (A : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) R)\n    (P Q : Finset (Fin n)) (h : P.card = Q.card) : R :=\n  (A.submatrix (P.orderEmbOfFin rfl) (Q.orderEmbOfFin (h ▸ rfl))).det",
          "theorem cauchyBinet {n m : ℕ} (A : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin m) R) (B : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin n) R) :\n    (A * B).det = ∑ S ∈ (Finset.univ : Finset (Fin m)).powersetCard n,\n      if h : S.card = n then\n        (colsSubmatrix A S h).det * (rowsSubmatrix B S h).det\n      else 0 := by\n  -- Expand det(AB) using Leibniz formula\n  calc (A * B).det \n      = ∑ σ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n), (Equiv.Perm.sign σ : R) * ∏ i, (A * B) (σ i) i := by \n          rw [Matrix.det_apply']\n    _ = ∑ σ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n), (Equiv.Perm.sign σ : R) * \n          ∏ i, ∑ k : Fin m, A (σ i) k * B k i := by rfl\n    _ = ∑ σ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n), (Equiv.Perm.sign σ : R) * \n          ∑ f : Fin n → Fin m, ∏ i, A (σ i) (f i) * B (f i) i := by\n        congr 1; ext σ; congr 1\n        rw [Finset.prod_univ_sum]; simp only [Fintype.piFinset_univ]\n    _ = ∑ σ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n), ∑ f : Fin n → Fin m, \n          (Equiv.Perm.sign σ : R) * ∏ i, A (σ i) (f i) * B (f i) i := by\n        congr 1; ext σ; rw [mul_sum]\n    _ = ∑ f : Fin n → Fin m, ∑ σ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n), \n          (Equiv.Perm.sign σ : R) * ∏ i, A (σ i) (f i) * B (f i) i := by\n        rw [Finset.sum_comm]\n    _ = ∑ f ∈ (Finset.univ : Finset (Fin n → Fin m)).filter Function.Injective, \n          ∑ σ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n), (Equiv.Perm.sign σ : R) * ∏ i, A (σ i) (f i) * B (f i) i := by\n        refine (Finset.sum_subset (Finset.filter_subset _ _) ?_).symm\n        intro f _ hf\n        simp only [Finset.mem_filter, Finset.mem_univ, true_and] at hf\n        exact det_mul_aux_nonsquare hf\n    _ = _ := by\n        -- Use the bijection between injective functions and (subset, permutation) pairs\n        rw [sum_injective_eq_sum_over_subsets]\n        apply Finset.sum_congr rfl; intro S hS\n        split_ifs with h\n        · exact sum_over_subset_eq_det_mul A B S h\n        · rfl"
        ]
      },

          "labels": [
            "lem.sf.alternant-0"
          ],
          "snippet": "\\begin{lemma}\n\\label{lem.sf.alternant-0}Let $\\alpha\\in\\mathbb{N}^{N}$. \\medskip\n\n\\textbf{(a)} If the $N$-tuple $\\alpha$ has two equal entries, then $a_{\\alpha\n}=0$. \\medskip\n\n\\textbf{(b)} Let $\\beta\\in\\mathbb{N}^{N}$ be an $N$-tuple obtained from\n$\\alpha$ by swapping two entries. Then, $a_{\\beta}=-a_{\\alpha}$.\n\\end{lemma}\n\n\\begin{proof}\n[Proof of Lemma \\ref{lem.sf.alternant-0}.]This is an easy consequence of\nDefinition \\ref{def.sf.alternants} \\textbf{(b)}. See Section\n\\ref{sec.details.sf.schur} for a detailed proof.\n\\end{proof}\n\n\\begin{proof}\n[Proof of Lemma \\ref{lem.sf.stemb-lem}.]For any $\\beta\\in\\mathbb{N}^{N}$ and\nany $i\\in\\left[  N\\right]  $, we let $\\beta_{i}$ denote the $i$-th entry of\n$\\beta$. Thus, for example, $\\rho_{k}=N-k$ for each $k\\in\\left[  N\\right]  $\n(since $\\rho=\\left(  N-1,N-2,\\ldots,N-N\\right)  $).\n\nSince addition on $\\mathbb{N}^{N}$ is defined entrywise, we have $\\left(\n\\beta+\\gamma\\right)  _{i}=\\beta_{i}+\\gamma_{i}$ for any $\\beta,\\gamma\n\\in\\mathbb{N}^{N}$ and $i\\in\\left[  N\\right]  $.\n\nThe group $S_{N}$ acts on $\\mathcal{P}$ by $K$-algebra automorphisms. Hence,\nin particular, we have%\n\\[\n\\sigma\\cdot\\left(  fg\\right)  =\\left(  \\sigma\\cdot f\\right)  \\cdot\\left(\n\\sigma\\cdot g\\right)\n\\]\nfor any $\\sigma\\in S_{N}$ and any $f,g\\in\\mathcal{P}$. In other words, we have%\n\\begin{equation}\n\\left(  \\sigma\\cdot f\\right)  \\cdot\\left(  \\sigma\\cdot g\\right)  =\\sigma\n\\cdot\\left(  fg\\right)  \\label{pf.lem.sf.stemb-lem.sigfg=}%\n\\end{equation}\nfor any $\\sigma\\in S_{N}$ and any $f,g\\in\\mathcal{P}$.\n"
        }
      ],

This equals the determinant-based\n    definition det(x_j^{α_i}) by `AlgebraicCombinatorics.alternant_eq_det`.\n\n    The explicit `N` parameter is kept for backward compatibility with existing code. -/\nnoncomputable abbrev alternant (N : ℕ) (α : Fin N → ℕ) : MvPolynomial (Fin N) R :=\n  AlgebraicCombinatorics.alternant α\n\n-- Predecessor context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/LittlewoodRichardson.lean:405-406\nnoncomputable abbrev xPow (α : Fin N → ℕ) : MvPolynomial (Fin N) R :=\n  AlgebraicCombinatorics.SymmetricFunctions.monomialExp α\n\n-- Predecessor context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/LittlewoodRichardson.lean:859-867\n/-- The alternant a_α = ∑_{σ ∈ S_N} sign(σ) · x^(σ·α).\n    Here σ·α means (α_{σ(1)}, α_{σ(2)}, ..., α_{σ(N)}).\n\n    **Equivalence with SchurBasics.alternant:**\n    This sum-based definition equals the determinant-based definition in\n    `alternant` (from SchurBasics.lean). See `alternant_eq_det` for the proof\n    that this equals `det(alternantMatrix α)`. -/\nnoncomputable def alternant (α : Fin N → ℕ) : MvPolynomial (Fin N) R :=\n  ∑ σ : Equiv.Perm (Fin N), Equiv.Perm.sign σ • xPow (α ∘ σ)\n\n-- Predecessor context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/LittlewoodRichardson.lean:1045-1099\n-- Local API retrieval: AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/SchurBasics.lean:154-157 (alternantTextbook)\ndef alternantTextbook (N : ℕ) (α : Fin N → ℕ) : MvPolynomial (Fin N) R :=\n  Matrix.det (Matrix.of fun i j => (X i : MvPolynomial (Fin N) R) ^ α j)\n\nomit [NeZero N] in\n-- Local API retrieval: AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/SchurBasics.lean:161-167 (alternantTextbook_eq_alternant)\ntheorem alternantTextbook_eq_alternant (α : Fin N → ℕ) :\n    alternantTextbook (R := R) N α = alternant N α := by\n  unfold alternantTextbook alternant\n  rw [AlgebraicCombinatorics.alternant_eq_det]\n  unfold AlgebraicCombinatorics.alternantMatrix\n  rw [← Matrix.det_transpose]\n  congr 1",

        "target_declaration_name_available_to_model": false,
        "grading_uses_withheld_gold_statement": true,

      }
    }
  ]
}
